The only reason that I got an iPhone was because of video follow up. So on an Android device, you don't have the ability to send a video text message to somebody. And if you do, you have the ability to send it but it comes through the lowest quality, lower than what a pixeled iPhone that in the early 2000s would look like. You were able to bring up video back then. It looks like something that's about 140p if you're familiar with the 1080p in a 4K system on how that range works. Literally, the lowest quality in the entire world if you send it on an Android, don't argue with me. By an iPhone, that's the only reason I got one. Literally to be able to send video messages. That being said, I went out of my way whenever I would make a call or create a communication with a potential client to send a video message. Let me give you an idea of what that might look like. You get off the phone with somebody. Okay, goodbye. You pull out your phone. You open up your text messages. You want to create a text to the individual you're sending the message to. Go ahead and click on the photo button there. Looks like I need to type somebody in first. One, two, three. Okay, cool. So you want to click on your photo button. You want to go over here, swipe to the video, turn it around. You want to have good lighting. So when you create a selfie video, which you can see here, all you want to do is just make sure that your face has even light on it so you look good. But after you do your video, the selfie point is, hey, what's going on? It was awesome speaking to you on the phone. I just wanted to put a face to the name or if you're speaking to somebody through the computer, you'd say, hey, it was awesome speaking to you on the computer. It was awesome talking to you messenger because you can also send these to you messenger. Anyway, I just wanted to put a face to the name and I wanted to make sure that you knew who I was. And just to let you know, I'm super excited for the follow up conversation that we were going to have. Anyway, I look forward to talking with you soon. Bye. That's it. End your video. Send it off. You can do these things on iPhone up to about a minute, I believe, where it sends doing a high quality. Anyway, moral story, ladies and gentlemen, whatever it is that you're doing in the field, follow it up with a video, whether it be the clothes that you just made to say that you're excited to start work or whether it be the fact that you're just excited for the follow of conversation that you just laid down. Whatever it is, ladies and gentlemen, send a video. See you next week.
